# Guidr — Complete Buenos Aires City Guide

> Source: https://guidr.info
> Languages: Spanish (es), English (en), Portuguese (pt)
> Focus: Puerto Madero, San Telmo, La Boca, Central Buenos Aires

---

## GASTRONOMÍA — Where to eat near Puerto Madero

### Cabaña Las Lilas
- Address: Av. Alicia Moreau de Justo 516, Puerto Madero
- Type: Premium parrilla (steakhouse)
- Description: The most famous premium steakhouse in Puerto Madero. Select cuts of Argentine beef with dock views. A quintessential Buenos Aires dining experience.
- Map: Search "Cabaña Las Lilas Puerto Madero"

### El Bodegón de Madero
- Address: Juana Manso 1300, Puerto Madero
- Type: Traditional bodegón
- Description: Classic Buenos Aires bodegón with generous portions, homemade pastas, and traditional Argentine dishes in a warm, unpretentious setting.

### Sagardi
- Address: Juana Manso 1167, Puerto Madero
- Type: Basque cuisine
- Description: Authentic Basque cuisine with pintxos, grilled meats, and top-quality produce. Spanish tradition, fire, and a celebration of food.

### Siga La Vaca
- Address: Av. Alicia Moreau de Justo 1714, Puerto Madero
- Type: All-you-can-eat parrilla
- Description: Classic all-you-can-eat parrilla with traditional grilled meats and plentiful sides in a relaxed setting. Great value.

### Café San Juan
- Address: Av. San Juan 450, San Telmo
- Type: Creative cuisine
- Description: Chef-driven creative cuisine in San Telmo. Inventive dishes with fresh produce, warm atmosphere, and a curated wine list.

### La Parolaccia
- Address: Av. Alicia Moreau de Justo 1052, Puerto Madero
- Type: Italian
- Description: Premium Italian cuisine on the Puerto Madero docks. Fresh pastas, seafood, and an extensive wine list.

### Mercado de San Telmo
- Address: Defensa 961, San Telmo
- Type: Historic market
- Description: Historic market with fruit, vegetable, butcher, antiques, and food stalls. An unmissable local experience for foodies and culture lovers.

---

## TANGO — Authentic milongas in Buenos Aires

### La Flor de Barracas
- Neighborhood: Barracas, Buenos Aires
- Type: Traditional milonga
- Description: A classic neighborhood milonga in Barracas. Original floors from the late 1800s, authentic atmosphere, where locals dance tango as it was meant to be — not for tourists, but for the love of the dance.
- Guide: https://guidr.info/es/guias/la-flor-de-barracas

### Los Laureles
- Neighborhood: Buenos Aires
- Type: Traditional milonga
- Description: A traditional milonga with strong neighborhood roots. Regular dancers, classic tango music, and an intimate setting that welcomes newcomers.
- Guide: https://guidr.info/es/guias/los-laureles

### Madero Tango
- Address: Puerto Madero
- Type: Dinner show
- Description: A polished tango dinner show in Puerto Madero. Professional dancers, live orchestra, and Argentine cuisine. The most accessible tango experience for visitors, right in the neighborhood.
- Guide: https://guidr.info/es/guias/madero-tango

---

## BARES — Best bars near Puerto Madero

### Bar Británico
- Address: Defensa 399, San Telmo
- Description: Legendary corner café in San Telmo, operating since 1928. Literary history, great coffee, and a window into old Buenos Aires.

### Bar Sur
- Address: Estados Unidos 299, San Telmo
- Description: Intimate live tango bar in San Telmo. Nightly shows in a small, atmospheric venue. One of the most authentic tango bar experiences.

### La Puerta Roja
- Address: Chacabuco 733, San Telmo
- Description: Hidden speakeasy-style bar behind a red door. Creative cocktails, eclectic crowd, and a secret garden patio.

### Temple Craft Beer
- Address: Puerto Madero
- Description: Craft beer bar with a wide selection of Argentine microbrews. Casual atmosphere, good for groups.

---

## BUENOS AIRES — Must-see landmarks

### Casa Rosada
- Address: Balcarce 50, Microcentro
- Description: The iconic pink presidential palace on Plaza de Mayo. Free guided tours available. Symbol of Argentine political history.

### Teatro Colón
- Address: Cerrito 628, Microcentro
- Description: One of the world's finest opera houses. Stunning architecture, incredible acoustics. Guided tours daily; check schedule for performances.

### Caminito, La Boca
- Description: The colorful street museum in La Boca. Tango dancers, street art, and the spirit of Buenos Aires. Visit during daytime only.

### Puente de la Mujer
- Address: Puerto Madero
- Description: Santiago Calatrava's iconic rotating footbridge. A Puerto Madero landmark, beautiful at sunset.

### Recoleta Cemetery
- Description: Monumental cemetery with elaborate mausoleums. Final resting place of Eva Perón. Architectural masterpiece.

### MALBA
- Description: Museum of Latin American Art. World-class collection of modern and contemporary Latin American art.

### Reserva Ecológica
- Address: Av. Tristán Achával Rodríguez 1550
- Description: 350-hectare nature reserve on the riverfront, adjacent to Puerto Madero. Walking trails, birdwatching, river views. Free entry.

---

## TRANSPORTE — Getting around Buenos Aires

### From Aeroparque (domestic airport)
- Distance to Puerto Madero: 20-25 minutes
- Taxi: ~USD 15
- Uber: Available (use pickup point)
- Transfer: Desks in arrivals hall

### From Ezeiza (international airport)
- Distance to Puerto Madero: 30-45 minutes
- Taxi: ~USD 25
- Uber: Available
- Transfer: ~USD 30 at official desks

### Getting around the city
- Subte (metro): 6 lines, covers central Buenos Aires. Buy SUBE card at any kiosk.
- Taxi: Yellow and black cabs, use meter. Safe and affordable.
- Uber / Cabify / DiDi: All available and widely used.
- Tourist bus: Hop-on hop-off covering major landmarks.

---

## EMERGENCIAS — Emergency numbers in Buenos Aires

- Police: 911
- Fire: 100
- Ambulance (SAME): 107

---

## FÚTBOL — Football experiences

### La Bombonera (Boca Juniors)
- Neighborhood: La Boca
- Description: The legendary home of Boca Juniors. One of the most intense football atmospheres in the world.

### Estadio Monumental (River Plate)
- Neighborhood: Núñez
- Description: Argentina's largest stadium, home of River Plate. Capacity 84,000. Historic venue of the 1978 World Cup final.

### River Experience
- Description: Official River Plate museum and stadium tour. Interactive exhibits, trophy room, and pitch access.

---

*Last updated: April 2026*
